frameworks.name: model-based planning
frameworks.one_line: agents use learned state transition functions to plan over sequential decisions

frameworks.name: model sharing / generalization across contexts
frameworks.one_line: transition functions reused across similar contexts to curb exponential memory cost

frameworks.name: reinforcement learning
frameworks.one_line: sequential decision making framed in terms of state, action, and outcome

core_question: Do humans share learned state transition models across contexts when context structure permits, and where in the brain is this implemented?
key_assumptions: planning depth amplifies memory demand exponentially in rich environments
state transition functions can be reused across contexts with shared structure
behavior and BOLD patterns can index model reuse
manipulations: context structure designed so transition knowledge could be shared in some contexts but not others
sign of received outcome (positive vs negative)
key_findings: computational modelling showed participants shared a transition model across contexts where appropriate
medial temporal lobe BOLD tracked learning of state transitions
BOLD patterns in MTL correlated across contexts that shared transition information
transition model was updated more strongly after positive than negative outcomes
interpretations: the human MTL implements context-sensitive sharing of state transition models for planning
model updating is asymmetric, weighted toward positive outcomes
